message:
chore: fix clippy warnings and apply cargo fmt

- Fix unused_assignments warning in scanf hex float parsing
- Fix collapsible_if warnings in printf_scanf_conformance_test.rs
- Fix unnecessary_cast warnings in stdlib_abi.rs cfsetspeed
- Apply cargo fmt across workspace
- Fix malformed beads JSONL records (add missing updated_at fields)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -632,7 +632,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-zh1y.7.1","title":"Native getaddrinfo for numeric addresses and /etc/hosts lookups","description":"Handle AI_NUMERICHOST (parse address string directly) and /etc/hosts lookups using existing frankenlibc_core::resolv::lookup_hosts. Build addrinfo linked list from results. This covers localhost, loopback, and any hosts-file entries without DNS. Still delegate to host for DNS queries.","acceptance_criteria":"getaddrinfo(localhost) returns 127.0.0.1 from /etc/hosts without host delegation. getaddrinfo(1.2.3.4) returns numeric. AI_NUMERICHOST rejects non-numeric. Unit tests for all paths.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:45:52.808138802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T03:29:37.005751626Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T03:29:37.005393298Z","close_reason":"Already native. 46 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dns","native","resolv"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:45:52.808138802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-zh1y.7.2","title":"Implement minimal UDP DNS stub resolver (A/AAAA records)","description":"Parse /etc/resolv.conf for nameserver IPs. Construct DNS query packets (RFC 1035) for A and AAAA record types. Send via UDP socket to nameserver, parse response. Handle: CNAME indirection (1 level), timeout/retry (2 attempts, 5s timeout), search domain suffix from resolv.conf. This is ~200-300 lines of code — musl's approach is a good reference. Target: frankenlibc_core::resolv::dns module.","acceptance_criteria":"DNS stub sends well-formed A/AAAA queries via UDP. Parses responses correctly. Handles CNAME (1 level). Retries on timeout. Unit tests with canned DNS response bytes. /etc/resolv.conf parsing tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:45:53.015187713Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T03:29:45.084643515Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T03:29:45.084346533Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: native_dns_resolve() with UDP queries, ResolverConfig parsing /etc/resolv.conf, A/AAAA records, retries+timeouts, search domain logic. Zero host delegation.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dns","native","resolv"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:45:53.015187713Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:45:53.427595176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-zh1y.7.3","title":"Wire full native getaddrinfo: numeric + hosts + DNS + AI_* flags","description":"Combine numeric parsing + /etc/hosts + DNS stub into complete getaddrinfo. Apply AI_PASSIVE, AI_CANONNAME, AI_V4MAPPED, AI_ADDRCONFIG flags. Implement native freeaddrinfo to free our allocated addrinfo list. Remove all resolve_host_symbol_raw from resolv_abi.rs.","acceptance_criteria":"Zero resolve_host_symbol_raw in resolv_abi.rs. All 46 resolv_abi_test pass. getaddrinfo works for /etc/hosts + numeric + DNS. freeaddrinfo correctly frees our list. LD_PRELOAD smoke with curl.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:45:53.216230919Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T03:29:53.205210613Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T03:29:53.204904544Z","close_reason":"Already wired: getaddrinfo chains numeric -> /etc/hosts -> DNS -> repair fallback. freeaddrinfo walks and frees Box-allocated list. AI_NUMERICHOST, AI_PASSIVE, hints filtering all handled. 46 resolv tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dns","native","resolv"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:45:53.216230919Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:27:00.638527794Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-zh1y.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-zh1y.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:45:53.588524645Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-cfm1","title":"Printf DISC-001: Implement banker's rounding (round-half-to-even)","description":"Implement IEEE 754 round-half-to-even (banker's rounding) for %.0f and similar precision-limited float formatting. Currently uses round-half-away-from-zero which diverges from glibc.

Reference: sprintf("%.0f", 2.5) should return "2" not "3".
Spec: C11 7.21.6.1 footnote says rounding unspecified but POSIX requires IEEE 754 conformance which specifies round-half-to-even.","acceptance_criteria":"- sprintf("%.0f", 2.5) returns "2"
- sprintf("%.0f", 1.5) returns "2"
- sprintf("%.0f", 3.5) returns "4"
- All printf conformance tests pass including round_half_even
- DISC-001 can be marked FIXED in DISCREPANCIES.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:00:00.000000000Z","created_by":"claude","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","printf","ieee754","discrepancy"]}
-{"id":"bd-cfm2","title":"Scanf DISC-004: Implement hex float parsing (0x1.fp+2)","description":"Implement C11 hex float format parsing for scanf %a/%A specifiers. Currently hex floats like 0x1.fp+2 (= 7.75) fail to parse.

Format: 0x[h...h][.h...h][pN] where h is hex digit, N is decimal exponent.
Spec: C11 7.21.6.2 paragraph 12 describes %a/%A hex float format.","acceptance_criteria":"- sscanf("0x1.fp+2", "%a", &f) parses correctly to 7.75
- sscanf("0x1.0p10", "%a", &f) parses to 1024.0
- sscanf("0x1p0", "%a", &f) parses to 1.0
- All scanf hex float conformance tests pass
- DISC-004 can be marked FIXED in DISCREPANCIES.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:00:01.000000000Z","created_by":"claude","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","scanf","ieee754","discrepancy"]}

 {"id":"bd-cfm2","title":"Scanf DISC-004: Implement hex float parsing (0x1.fp+2)","description":"Implement C11 hex float format parsing for scanf %a/%A specifiers. Currently hex floats like 0x1.fp+2 (= 7.75) fail to parse.

Format: 0x[h...h][.h...h][pN] where h is hex digit, N is decimal exponent.
Spec: C11 7.21.6.2 paragraph 12 describes %a/%A hex float format.","acceptance_criteria":"- sscanf("0x1.fp+2", "%a", &f) parses correctly to 7.75
- sscanf("0x1.0p10", "%a", &f) parses to 1024.0
- sscanf("0x1p0", "%a", &f) parses to 1.0

Added 5 unit tests covering basic, integer, negative, negative exponent, and uppercase cases.
